subject: Physics
title: Why Perpetual Motion Is Impossible
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Why Perpetual Motion Is Impossible.

hook.text: For centuries, inventors have tried to build machines that run forever. Every single one has failed.

buildup.text: A perpetual motion machine of the first kind creates energy from nothing—violating the first law.

discovery.text: A machine of the second kind converts heat entirely to work with no waste—violating the second law.

twist.text: The US Patent Office refuses to even review perpetual motion patents without a working model.

climax.text: Friction, air resistance, and entropy guarantee that every machine eventually runs down.

punchline.text: Run forever with no fuel? Two laws of physics say absolutely not.

quiz.question: Which law does a perpetual motion machine of the first kind violate?
options: The first law of thermodynamics (conservation of energy)
